notes:
Opening turn work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex12-20260630145920.

Objective: opening statement on proposition that by May 24, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace within the market rule.

Record inspection: get_case/list_evidence show only five initial case-packet files, all juror-visible case_packet evidence; no arguments, openings, submitted evidence, exhibits, or technical reports. Exact bytes read for all five files.

Evidence analysis:
- ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page supplies the operative rule. It proves the legal elements: Iran-initiated closure; non-weather; by listed date 11:59 PM ET; major closure meaning broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region, or non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). It also identifies primary resolution sources: official Iranian aviation authority information and consensus credible reporting. It does not prove the May 24 merits event.
- ev_72c545ccb705_official-source-record states no merits official-source packet exists yet and directs attorneys to official Iranian aviation authority records/NOTAMs/airport records/credible reporting. It proves the packet’s own gap and source priorities, not the event.
- ev_9f8061d601c0_primary-evidence states no merits evidence packet exists; identifies priority categories. It does not prove closure.
- ev_4a5f41c923ac_secondary-reporting states no merits secondary-reporting packet exists. It does not prove closure.
- ev_f994792e9085_unresolved-record-gaps lists gaps: no UMA rationales/event report; no Iranian authority/NOTAM/airport ops/contemporaneous reporting; no proof Iran initiation vs airlines/foreign governments; no proof broad enough; no exact ET timing. This is adverse to overclaiming in opening.

Source map: decisive elements are Iran initiation, non-weather reason, timing by 2026-05-24 23:59 ET, major closure breadth, commercial-flight effect, provenance/custody. Strong primary target is official CAO/CAA Iran NOTAM or notice, especially lead NOTAM OIIX A1010/26. Confirming targets: airport notices for IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN, credible reporting (Reuters/AP/major regional), and cautious flight tracking. Strong defense target: only western/partial restriction with exceptions or foreign/airline-initiated rerouting.

Native investigation before opening: web searches run for May 24 Iran airspace closure, CAO/CAA, NOTAM, western airspace, May 25. Search-result leads (not admitted) point to reports of CAA/CAO Iran NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22, 2026 restricting/closing western Tehran FIR through May 25 with exceptions/daylight operations and around eight airports. Indian Express/Gulf News/Siasat/Polymarket snippets mention the lead. These are not record proof. Adverse issue from same leads: event may be characterized as western/regional/partial with exceptions, so plaintiff later must prove it qualifies as major under “major Iranian airspace region” and/or listed-airport clause.

Opening decision: file a credible, forward-looking opening. Do not cite unadmitted web leads as proof. Explain that the packet fixes the rule and source targets; acknowledge current gaps; identify expected official NOTAM/authority records and corroborating reporting/airport records as decisive. Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening work notes, 2026-06-30. Opportunity openings:defendant; final action record_opening_statement; evidence submission unavailable. Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence and read all five case-packet files by read_evidence_range. Record has only market-page, primary-evidence, official-source-record, secondary-reporting, unresolved-record-gaps; no merits evidence/exhibits/submitted evidence. Market rule requires Iran-initiated, non-weather, by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET, major closure: broad commercial-flight closure/suspension across Iran or major Iranian airspace region, or complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Warnings/foreign restrictions/airline choices/limited cancellations/partial restrictions/weather do not qualify. Packet expressly says no merits official-source or secondary packet exists and lists gaps: no NOTAMs, Iranian authority records, airport operations records, contemporaneous credible reporting, UMA rationales, exact ET timing, causation by Iran vs airlines/foreign governments, or breadth determination.

Native investigation before opening: searched web for Iran May 24 2026 airspace NOTAM, site:caa.gov.ir, exact phrases, western Tehran FIR May 22 2026 NOTAM, OIIX May 22/May 25, sunrise-sunset. Leads: Polymarket snippets and several reports/social posts reference NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 or related A0796/26/A1015/26, dated May 22-25, western Tehran FIR, daylight-only/fresh approval exceptions; Defcon/Indian Express/Arab Times snippets report western FIR restriction and list several airport codes; Iran International snippet reports May 23 CAO denial of any new official aviation notice imposing countrywide restrictions and says flights normal. These outside leads are not admitted evidence and should not be cited as proof in opening. They shape later evidence targets and adverse checks only.

Theory: Defendant opening should stress burden discipline. Current record proves only the market rule/source hierarchy and that decisive merits evidence is missing. Plaintiff must submit admitted official Iranian authority/NOTAM or equivalent plus credible reporting fixing attribution, timing, non-weather cause, and breadth. A limited western FIR, daylight-only, permission-based, regional, airport-specific, or contradicted notice may not satisfy the rule, especially if it does not completely suspend arrivals/departures at at least two listed airports. Work journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with search ledger, source targets, and unresolved gaps.

Argument turn work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex12-20260630145920.

Record rescanned with get_case/list_evidence at start of opportunity. Visible before submissions: five initial case-packet items plus plaintiff and defendant openings; no submitted evidence from either side and no exhibits/reports.

Decisive elements: Iran initiation; non-weather reason; timing by 2026-05-24 23:59 ET; major closure breadth; commercial flight effect; provenance. Defense opening emphasizes missing official NOTAM, exact effect, scope/exceptions, and possible partial regional restriction.

Search and capture path: Continued from opening source map. Searched exact NOTAM identifier and source terms: "OIIX A1010/26", "A1010/26 OIIX NOTAM", "OIIX A1010/26 May 22" plus earlier May 24/western airspace/CAA queries. Official NOTAM/CAA source was not captured. Search results included social snippets/OCR and secondary reports. Opened and extracted Indian Express, Gulf News, Kenya Times, and Siasat. Chose to submit Indian Express, Gulf News, Kenya Times because they preserved the most useful corroborating details and/or direct page text.

Submitted evidence accepted:
- ev_09982b77bf9e_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-09982b77bf9e: Indian Express report. It reports Iranian Civil Aviation Authority NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, May 22-May 25, western Tehran FIR/western half, suspension of civil flight permits, all airports in western FIR shut, only eight airports operational under daylight restrictions and fresh per-flight CAA approval, and flight-tracking reroutes. Limit: secondary report; some details attributed to Gulf News; full NOTAM not reproduced.
- ev_9f6b49a1ebfa_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-9f6b49a1ebfa: Gulf News live update. It reports Iran CAA NOTAM restricting operations at most western airports in Tehran FIR until early May 25, limited daytime flights at eight sites, and possible operational/military-exercise/security context. Limit: secondary live update; no full NOTAM; acknowledges limited daytime flights.
- ev_47df93957e6e_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-47df93957e6e: Kenya Times report. It reports NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 effective May 22 20:46Z to May 25 08:30Z; all airports in western Tehran FIR closed except limited daylight sites; prior operator permissions suspended; fresh CAA approvals required for IFR passenger operations; flight tracking showed reroutes. Limit: secondary report, not official NOTAM; lists specific airport-code exceptions.

Analysis: Timing is strong: May 22 20:46Z equals May 22 16:46 ET, and May 25 08:30Z is after the May 24 23:59 ET cutoff. Initiation is supported by all three reports attributing NOTAM to Iran's CAA. Non-weather supported by security/military/diplomatic context and absence of weather rationale. Major is best argued under 'major Iranian airspace region' because the record shows western Tehran FIR restrictions and broad suspension of prior permissions; do not rely primarily on the two-listed-airport complete suspension prong due daylight exceptions and uncertainty. Adverse points: no official NOTAM captured; secondary reports have some inconsistency about the eight exceptions; restrictions were western/regional with exceptions; defense will call this partial. Plaintiff response: the rule expressly covers a major Iranian airspace region, and limited exceptions to a broad closure do not disqualify. Western Tehran FIR plus most western airports, cancellation of prior permissions, fresh approvals for passenger IFR, and widespread rerouting satisfy preponderance.

Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits work notes. Opportunity arguments:defendant; allowed submit_evidence and submit_argument. Inspected current record and evidence list. New plaintiff evidence: ev_09982b77bf9e Indian Express, ev_9f6b49a1ebfa Gulf News, ev_47df93957e6e Kenya Times; all are secondary text captures. Read exact contents with read_evidence_range. Plaintiff technical report concedes no official NOTAM database or Iranian authority page captured. Plaintiff evidence consistently reports NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, May 22-May 25, western Tehran FIR airport restrictions, daylight exceptions and fresh CAA approval, but reports conflict on airport lists: Indian Express says eight operational airports include IKA, THR, IFN, SYZ, MHD; Kenya Times lists OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW; Gulf News capture does not list them.

Defendant investigation: searched exact phrases and opened Iran International article https://www.iranintl.com/en/202605239595; captured and submitted as ev_4c36e105a2b8. It reports CAO denial of any new countrywide airspace notice/normal flight conditions, while acknowledging earlier western Tehran FIR airport suspension with limited exceptions. Searched A1010/26 OITR/OIKK and found Notamify public raw NOTAM page https://notamify.com/notams/OIIX/693f2d0f-54d3-433b-8128-49f5475ba269; captured and submitted as ev_342c71021810. Raw text: A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26 Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/ A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST E)REF NOTAM A0796/26, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW sunrise-sunset; previous permissions suspended; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports.

Analysis: raw NOTAM is double-edged and should be treated candidly. It strengthens plaintiff on timing/CAA/formality/non-weather, but helps defense on scope: QFALC and /A/ are aerodrome/airport closure indicators; E-line closes airports in west part of Tehran FIR, not the Tehran FIR airspace or transiting routes; exceptions and daylight approvals remain. Listed-airport prong not proven: raw exceptions include only OISS/SYZ among the listed airport codes, while Indian Express says all five listed airports were among airports remaining operational daylight-only, not completely suspended. Major-region prong is closer; defense argument should not deny a serious restriction, but should argue plaintiff has not shown preponderance of a rule-qualifying major airspace closure because official custody is missing and the best raw text is an airport-specific restriction with exceptions. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal turn work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex12-20260630145920.

Current opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Record reviewed from prompt and defense additions identified. Defendant added two evidence items: ev_342c71021810_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-342c71021810 (Notamify raw NOTAM mirror) and ev_4c36e105a2b8_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-4c36e105a2b8 (Iran International CAO denial report). Exact bytes read with read_evidence_range for both.

D-X1/Notamify content: Raw text states A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26; Q) OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A) OIIX; B) 2605222046; C) 2605250830EST; E) REF NOTAM A0796/26, ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR ARE CLSD EXCEPT OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, AND OIAW THAT ARE OPERATIONAL FROM SUNRISE TO SUNSET (HJ). ALL PREVIOUS PERMISSIONS ARE SUSPENDED FOR ALL OPERATORS, NEW PERMISSION SHALL BE REQUESTED FROM CAA FOR OPERATION OF CIVIL IFR PASSENGER FLIGHTS ON THESE AIRPORTS. Defense argues QFALC/A and airport wording means aerodrome closure, not airspace route closure.

D-X2/Iran International content: Report title says Iran denies new NOTAM limiting flights across country. It says CAO said no new official notice imposing restrictions on country's airspace and normal flights, but also states the denial came after CAO said Friday that activity at airports in western Tehran FIR had been suspended until Monday with only a limited number of airports allowed to operate; earlier notice listed Urmia, Kerman, Abadan, Shiraz, Yazd, Kermanshah, Rasht, Ahvaz exceptions sunrise-sunset.

Rebuttal analysis: Defense evidence strengthens provenance and timing. D-X1 confirms a formal NOTAM, west Tehran FIR, all airports closed except limited daylight list, all previous permissions suspended for all operators, and new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. Market rule is not limited to enroute closure; it covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region. Aerodrome closures and permission suspension for civil IFR passenger flights across west Tehran FIR airports are at minimum a broad suspension of commercial arrivals/departures in that region. Do not rely on listed-airport prong; maintain major-region prong. D-X2 is not a denial of the earlier western-FIR suspension; it denies a new countrywide notice and confirms the earlier western-FIR airport suspension. Limited exceptions do not disqualify a broad closure under the rule.

No new external evidence needed because defense evidence directly supplies the raw text and conflict context; adding more would risk overextending. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal notes. Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant; allowed submit_surrebuttal or pass. Inspected record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal added no new source evidence; it re-offered defendant's D-X1 raw NOTAM mirror and D-X2 Iran International report as PX-R1/PX-R2. Strongest plaintiff rebuttal: D-X1 confirms formal NOTAM A1010/26, May 22-May 25, all airports in west part Tehran FIR closed except daylight sites, all prior permissions suspended and new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights; plaintiff says this satisfies major-region prong and limited exceptions do not disqualify broad closure, abandoning listed-airport prong.

Analysis for surrebuttal: No new outside source is needed because rebuttal turns on legal/record reading of existing evidence. Need answer strongest point, not repeat merits. Emphasize that the limited-exception clause only saves an otherwise broad closure; it does not prove breadth. D-X1's precise object is airports, not enroute airspace or transiting commercial flights: QFALC /A/ and E-line says all airports in west part Tehran FIR closed except listed airports; new permission for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Plaintiff still lacks official NOTAM custody, direct authority page, airport operations records, or evidence that commercial flights transiting the region were suspended. D-X2 confirms the earlier western airport suspension but also reports CAO denial of a new countrywide airspace notice and normal flight conditions; use it to resist broader characterization. Journal updated.

Closing turn work notes, plaintiff, arb-ex12-20260630145920.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Final record scanned with get_case/list_evidence. Record now contains initial packet, plaintiff's submitted evidence (Indian Express, Gulf News, Kenya Times), defendant's submitted evidence (Notamify raw NOTAM mirror and Iran International denial/limitation article), plaintiff rebuttal, defendant surrebuttal, and technical reports. No new evidence may be submitted in closing.

Record analysis for closing:
- Market rule (ev_6c99672d9a2c) permits Yes for major closure defined as broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region. Limited exceptions do not disqualify. Separate listed-airport route exists but plaintiff should not rely on it in closing.
- Plaintiff evidence (ev_09982..., ev_9f6..., ev_47df...) consistently reports Iran CAA/CAA NOTAM A1010/26, May 22-May 25, western Tehran FIR/western airports, daylight exceptions, fresh approvals, security/non-weather context.
- Defendant Notamify mirror (ev_342...) is strongest record item. It provides raw NOTAM wording: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except eight named daylight exceptions; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new permission from CAA required for civil IFR passenger flights; effective B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST. This confirms timing before and through cutoff, Iran/CAA permission regime, and broad commercial passenger operational effect.
- Defendant Iran International report (ev_4c36...) denies a new countrywide airspace notice but acknowledges earlier CAO statement that activity at western Tehran FIR airports had been suspended until Monday with only limited airports allowed sunrise-sunset. This undercuts countrywide overclaim but confirms the major-region suspension.
- Defense strongest point: QFALC/A and E-line are airport/aerodrome notice, not enroute airspace route closure. Plaintiff answer: rule is not enroute-only; it covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights arriving/departing from a major Iranian airspace region. Commercial passenger flights cannot operate normally where all regional airports are closed except limited daylight sites and all prior permissions are suspended pending CAA reauthorization. Limited exceptions are expressly allowed.
- Weight limits to concede: no official NOTAM database capture; Notamify mirror is not official custody; exceptions exist; listed-airport complete-suspension prong not established. These do not defeat preponderance.

Closing decision: Argue Yes under major-region prong. Keep concise, synthesize decisive evidence, confront defense scope point and provenance limitations.

Defendant closing notes. Opportunity closings:defendant; only deliver_closing_statement. Inspected current record and evidence list. Plaintiff closing added no evidence; it argues D-X1 is strongest proof and that airport closures plus CAA permission regime satisfy major-region prong because commercial flights arrive/depart through airports and limited exceptions do not disqualify broad closure. No new source work allowed/needed in closing. Closing plan: concede serious Iran-issued non-weather restriction and timing/authority evidence. Focus on exact proposition and rule: major closure of airspace or major airspace region, with transiting/arriving/departing commercial flights, or complete suspension at two listed airports. Plaintiff does not rely on listed-airport prong. Best raw text is QFALC /A/ airport/aerodrome notice: all airports in west part Tehran FIR closed except eight daylight airports with new permission for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. No official NOTAM database/authority page, no airport operations records, inconsistent secondary airport lists, no direct evidence of enroute/transiting ban or actual region-wide commercial suspension. Ask for No because the record proves a serious airport restriction, not more likely than not a qualifying major airspace closure.
